title: Fix Candid opt variant encoding for role submission

requirements.text: In the frontend role submission logic, convert the plain string role value to the correct Candid opt variant format before sending to the backend. Specifically: map "customer" → [{ customer: null }], "driver" → [{ driver: null }], and "admin" → [{ admin: null }]. This conversion must be applied wherever a role is submitted to the backend (e.g., useQueries.ts updateRole mutation, SelectRolePage.tsx, RoleSelectionWarningModal.tsx).

quotes: Currently frontend is sending a string like: role: "customer"
If role is "customer" send: role: { customer: null }
Wrap it correctly inside opt: role: [{ customer: null }]

acceptanceCriteria: Submitting role 'customer' sends [{ customer: null }] to the backend, not the string "customer"
Submitting role 'driver' sends [{ driver: null }] to the backend
Submitting role 'admin' sends [{ admin: null }] to the backend
The role is wrapped in an array to represent the Candid opt type (e.g., [{ customer: null }])
No changes are made to auth logic, route guards, admin logic, booking, pricing, GPS, or UI design
Role selection from SelectRolePage and RoleSelectionWarningModal both use the corrected encoding
The backend no longer returns a Candid variant encoding error when a role is saved

constraints: Do not modify auth logic or Internet Identity hooks
Do not modify route guards (AuthGate, ProtectedRoute, RoleGuard)
Do not modify admin logic or AdminUpgradePage
Do not modify booking or trip-related logic
Do not modify pricing configuration logic
Do not modify GPS or location logic
Do not modify any UI design, layout, or styling
Only fix the role variant encoding mismatch in role submission calls
nonGoals: Changing backend Motoko types or schema
Modifying how roles are read or displayed (getRoleString helper)
Any new features or UI changes
